lyrics:
Step up to the mic
Dig in for the night
Ain't no place for fright
Just remember the lick

Throat burns inside out
Relax before the scream
What makes this a dream
Less certain than a doubt

In every face the crowd sways
And weaves and bobs, but never stays

Confused by your wires
Lost in the chords
A melody unfounded
Your harmony expires
No rewards
No rewards

Tuning out the static
I still can’t find the key
A beggar’s sympathy
Pretending I’m automatic

Fingers bleeding meaning
The amp can’t understand
The drummer breaks his hand
But no one hears him screaming

In every face the crowd sways
And weaves and bobs, but never stays

Confused by your wires
Lost in the chords
A melody unfounded
Your harmony expires
No rewards
No rewards

Every signal fades the same
Silence holds the longest note
And drowns the words I never wrote
When every name forgets its fame

In every face the crowd sways
And weaves and bobs, but never stays

Confused by your wires
Lost in the chords
A melody unfounded
Your harmony expires
No rewards
No rewards

No rewards
No rewards
No rewards
No rewards
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This is a warm, organic funk-rock track with a slightly tense yet soulful atmosphere, built around a core of electric guitar, bass, drums, and lead vocals. The energy is consistently high and driving, maintaining a confident, danceable feel that suggests a live performance. The production is clean and spacious, with each instrument occupying its own distinct place in the mix, creating a sound that is full without being dense. The track's defining quality is its virtuosic and expressive guitar work, which establishes the mood from the very beginning and culminates in fiery, intricate solos that elevate the song's energy.

review.review_text: PCBender writes the musician's anxiety dream better than most, and 'Confused by Your Wires' earns that territory honestly. The lyric 'the amp can't understand / the drummer breaks his hand / but no one hears him screaming' lands like a bruise, and in E minor at a pace that hits harder than you expect for a track this emotionally precise, the guitar work gives the alienation some real teeth. The chorus piles up those 'no rewards' like unpaid debts, and by the time it cycles back the third time, the repetition feels earned rather than lazy. A highlight.
anchors_used: lyric: 'the amp can't understand / the drummer breaks his hand / but no one hears him screaming'
